concept: Play / Engage in Recreation / Act Without Serious Purpose
description: To engage in activity for enjoyment rather than necessity—games, make-believe, spontaneous movement, creative exploration without stakes. 'Wile' names the essential lightness that balances serious effort, the space where learning happens through pleasure and where spirit refreshes itself.
sound_symbolism: The word is light and quick like play itself. 'Wi' begins with soft glide, movement easy and free. Then 'le' lifts with liquid release, the weight of seriousness set aside.
grammatical_notes: As verb: 'mi wile' (I play). As noun: 'wela wile' (good play / fun). Can take objects for games: 'mi themo wile' (I play something/a game). Distinct from riola (work); wile has no required outcome.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Play teaches non-attachment to outcomes—engaging fully while holding results lightly. Joy as practice.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Play is where creativity flows most freely—unconstrained by function, open to beauty for its own sake.
